title: Harbor Chili
description: A hearty, budget-friendly beef and bean chili built for busy family nights.
steps: Dice the onion and drain and rinse the kidney beans.
Brown the ground beef and onion in a large pot over medium heat, then drain excess grease.
Stir in the beans, diced tomatoes, tomato sauce, chili powder, and cumin.
Bring to a gentle boil, reduce the heat, and simmer uncovered for 25 to 30 minutes.
Taste and adjust seasoning, then serve with shredded cheddar if desired.

title: Cheesy Breakfast Quesadillas
description: Crisp tortillas filled with fluffy eggs, cheese, and optional breakfast sausage.
steps: Cook and crumble the sausage in a skillet if using, then transfer it to a plate.
Whisk the eggs and scramble them gently in the same skillet with half the butter.
Lay out the tortillas and divide the eggs, sausage, and cheese over one half of each tortilla.
Fold each tortilla closed and cook with the remaining butter until golden on both sides.
Slice into wedges and serve warm with salsa.

title: Chicken Ranch Wraps
description: Fast chicken wraps with crisp lettuce, cheddar, and creamy ranch for an easy lunch.
steps: Chop the cooked chicken, lettuce, and tomato into bite-size pieces.
Warm the tortillas briefly so they fold without tearing.
Divide the chicken, lettuce, tomato, and cheddar between the tortillas.
Drizzle each wrap with ranch dressing.
Fold in the sides, roll tightly, and cut in half before serving.

title: Fudgy Budget Brownies
description: Simple cocoa brownies with chewy edges and a rich chocolate center.
steps: Heat the oven to 350°F and grease an 8-inch square baking pan.
Melt the butter and stir in the sugar until glossy.
Whisk in the eggs and vanilla.
Fold in the flour, cocoa powder, and salt just until no dry streaks remain.
Fold in chocolate chips if using, spread the batter in the pan, and bake for 24 to 28 minutes.
Cool before slicing so the center can set.

title: Cinnamon Sugar Dessert Nachos
description: Crispy cinnamon tortilla chips with chocolate drizzle for a fast late-night sweet snack.
steps: Heat the oven to 375°F and line a baking sheet.
Cut each tortilla into wedges and brush both sides lightly with melted butter.
Mix the sugar and cinnamon, then sprinkle it evenly over the tortilla wedges.
Bake for 8 to 10 minutes until crisp and lightly golden.
Melt the chocolate chips in short microwave bursts and drizzle over the warm chips.
Serve immediately with whipped cream if desired.

title: Teriyaki Chicken Bowls
description: Sweet and savory glazed chicken served over rice with broccoli and carrots for an easy family bowl night.
steps: Cook the rice according to the package directions and keep it warm.
Cut the chicken into bite-size pieces and pat it dry.
Heat the oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat and cook the chicken for 6 to 8 minutes until browned and cooked through.
Pour in the teriyaki sauce and simmer for 3 to 4 minutes until the chicken is glossy and coated.
Steam the broccoli and carrots until crisp-tender.
Divide the rice among bowls, add the vegetables and chicken, and finish with green onions and sesame seeds if desired.

title: Creamy Garlic Chicken Pasta
description: Tender chicken and pasta tossed in a rich, family-friendly garlic Parmesan sauce that comes together in about 30 minutes.
steps: Cook the pasta in salted water until al dente, reserving 1 cup of pasta water before draining.
Slice the chicken into cutlets and season both sides with Italian seasoning, salt, and pepper.
Melt 1 tablespoon of butter in a large skillet and cook the chicken for 4 to 5 minutes per side until cooked through, then rest and slice it.
Add the remaining butter and minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 30 seconds.
Whisk in the chicken broth and cream cheese until smooth, then stir in the Parmesan.
Toss the pasta with the sauce, adding reserved pasta water as needed, and top with sliced chicken and parsley.

title: Beef and Broccoli Stir-Fry
description: Tender beef and crisp broccoli in a savory homemade sauce, served over rice for a takeout-inspired family dinner.
steps: Cook the rice according to the package directions.
Slice the steak thinly against the grain and cut the broccoli into bite-size florets.
Whisk together the soy sauce, brown sugar, cornstarch, minced garlic, and 1 cup of water.
Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a large skillet and stir-fry the broccoli for 4 minutes, then transfer it to a plate.
Add the remaining oil and cook the beef in batches until browned.
Return the broccoli to the skillet, pour in the sauce, and simmer for 2 to 3 minutes until thickened. Serve over rice with green onions and sesame seeds.

title: Chicken and Dumplings
description: A cozy pot of tender chicken, vegetables, and fluffy drop dumplings in a creamy seasoned broth.
steps: Dice the carrots, celery, and onion.
Melt 2 tablespoons of butter in a Dutch oven and cook the vegetables for 6 to 8 minutes until softened.
Stir in 0.25 cup flour and the thyme, then slowly add the chicken broth while stirring.
Add the shredded chicken and simmer gently for 10 minutes.
Whisk the remaining 2 cups flour with the baking powder and a pinch of salt, then stir in the milk and remaining melted butter to make a soft dough.
Drop spoonfuls of dough over the simmering broth, cover, and cook without lifting the lid for 15 minutes until the dumplings are cooked through.

title: Chicken Casserole
description: A creamy chicken, broccoli, and rice casserole with cheddar and a buttery cracker topping for an easy family dinner.
steps: Heat the oven to 375°F and grease a 9-by-13-inch baking dish.
Steam the broccoli for 3 to 4 minutes until just tender.
Mix the chicken, rice, broccoli, soup, sour cream, garlic powder, and 1.5 cups of cheddar in a large bowl.
Spread the mixture evenly in the baking dish and top with the remaining cheddar.
Combine the crushed crackers with melted butter and scatter them over the casserole.
Bake uncovered for 30 to 35 minutes until bubbling and golden, then rest for 5 minutes before serving.

title: Garlic Butter Shrimp and Rice
description: Juicy shrimp in a buttery garlic sauce served over fluffy rice for a quick coastal-inspired family dinner.
steps: Rinse the rice and cook it in the chicken broth according to the package directions.
Pat the shrimp dry and season lightly with salt and pepper.
Melt 2 tablespoons of butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat and cook the shrimp for 1 to 2 minutes per side until pink, then transfer them to a plate.
Reduce the heat to medium and add the remaining butter, minced garlic, and red pepper flakes if using. Cook for 30 seconds.
Return the shrimp to the skillet and toss with the garlic butter, lemon juice, and parsley.
Fluff the rice, spoon the shrimp and garlic butter over it, and serve with lemon wedges.

title: Harbor Breakfast
description: A classic family breakfast plate with creamy scrambled eggs, crisp bacon, buttered toast, and fresh fruit.
steps: Cook the bacon in a large skillet or in a 400°F oven until crisp, then drain it on paper towels.
Whisk the eggs with the milk, a pinch of salt, and black pepper.
Melt 1 tablespoon of butter in a nonstick skillet over medium-low heat.
Add the eggs and stir gently until soft curds form and the eggs are just set.
Toast the bread and spread it with the remaining butter.
Divide the eggs, bacon, toast, and berries among four plates and serve warm.

title: Harbor Breakfast French Toast
description: Golden cinnamon French toast topped with berries, bananas, and maple syrup for a cozy family breakfast.
steps: Whisk the eggs, milk, vanilla, cinnamon, and a pinch of salt in a shallow dish.
Heat a griddle or large skillet over medium heat and melt a little butter across the surface.
Dip each bread slice in the egg mixture, coating both sides without soaking it too long.
Cook the bread in batches for 2 to 3 minutes per side until golden brown, adding more butter as needed.
Keep finished slices warm while cooking the remaining batches.
Serve with maple syrup, berries, bananas, and powdered sugar if desired.

title: Harbor Breakfast Skillet
description: A hearty one-pan breakfast with crispy potatoes, smoked sausage, peppers, cheddar, and sunny eggs.
steps: Dice the potatoes, sausage, bell peppers, and onion into bite-size pieces.
Heat the oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium heat and cook the potatoes for 12 to 15 minutes until browned and nearly tender.
Add the sausage, peppers, and onion and cook for 6 to 8 minutes until the vegetables soften.
Sprinkle the cheddar over the skillet and make six small wells in the mixture.
Crack an egg into each well, cover, and cook over medium-low heat for 5 to 7 minutes until the egg whites are set.
Finish with green onions and serve with sliced avocado if desired.

title: Chocolate Mug Cake
description: A warm single-serving chocolate cake mixed and cooked right in a mug for a fast late-night treat.
steps: In a 12-ounce microwave-safe mug, whisk together the flour, sugar, cocoa powder, baking powder, and salt.
Add the milk, vegetable oil, and vanilla and stir until the batter is smooth with no dry pockets.
Fold in the chocolate chips, reserving a few for the top.
Microwave on high for 60 to 75 seconds, stopping when the top looks set but the center is still soft.
Let the cake cool for 1 minute before eating it from the mug.
